62f3ed6a57b6d50239b52184.question_sanitized: This painter might have used engravings of the Roman Colosseum as models for his two versions of The Tower of Babel. This artist imitated the imagery of Hieronymus Bosch in the depiction of demented creatures in his The Fall of the Rebel Angels. Just the green legs of the title figure can be seen above the water as an ignorant plowman dominates the center in this painter's Landscape with the Fall of Icarus. For 10 points, Peasant Wedding and Hunters in the Snow are works by what Dutch painter?

62f3ed6a57b6d50239b5219b.leadin: Robert Mapplethorpe’s works in this medium caused Senator Jesse Helms to call for ending funding for the National Endowment of the Arts. For 10 points each:
62f3ed6a57b6d50239b5219b.leadin_sanitized: Robert Mapplethorpe's works in this medium caused Senator Jesse Helms to call for ending funding for the National Endowment of the Arts. For 10 points each:
parts: Mapplethorpe’s frank depictions of homoerotic and BDSM content were made in what visual art medium, as was his cover art for Patti Smith’s record Horses?
Mapplethorpe made most of his money selling still lifes of these natural items, including Calla Lilies.

Mapplethorpe photographed the artist Louise Bourgeois, who may be best known for her sculptures of these creatures, a large one of which called “Maman” stands outside the Guggenheim Bilbao next to Jeff Koons’s Puppy.
